process_name: Short-lived neutral-kaon rare semileptonic electron mode

grep_commands: rg -n "K0S|K_S|KS|K\(S\)|pi0 e|pi\^0 e|pi0e|S012\.10|a_S|a_\{S\}|s\s*->\s*d.*e|DeltaS = 1|weak neutral current|rare kaon|semileptonic" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\*|R_K|R_D|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e|K_L|K0L|K\+.*pi" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:755: evaluate_delta_mk implements K_L-K_S mass-difference NP evaluation.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(K_S\to\pi^0 e^+e^-)\).
This entry covers the short-lived neutral-kaon rare semileptonic mode
\(K_S\to\pi^0 e^+e^-\), a \(\Delta S=1\) weak-neutral-current decay.  It is
the electron-mode \(K_S\) companion to
\(K008\) because it fixes the indirect-CP input used in \(K_L\to\pi^0 e^+e^-\)
phenomenology.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical PDG 2026 pdgLive listing \(S012.10\), accessed 2026-05-16, gives
the NA48/1 measurement
\[
  \mathcal{B}(K_S\to\pi^0 e^+e^-;\,m_{ee}>0.165~{\rm GeV})
  = \left(3.0^{+1.5}_{-1.2}\,{\rm stat}\pm0.2\,{\rm syst}\right)
    \times10^{-9}.
\]
The same listing records the NA48/1 full-kinematic extrapolation, using a
constant form factor and vector matrix element,
\[
  \mathcal{B}(K_S\to\pi^0 e^+e^-)
  = \left(5.8^{+2.9}_{-2.4}\right)\times10^{-9}.
\]
Both values trace to \texttt{pdg2026\_ks\_pi0ee\_pdgLive.txt} in the YAML
\texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent} block.  The NA48/1 arXiv snapshot reports seven
observed events with an expected background of \(0.15\) events
(\texttt{na48\_2003\_ks\_pi0ee\_arxiv.txt}).
sections.relevance:
This mode probes \(s\to d e^+e^-\) semileptonic neutral-current structure,
not the \(\Delta F=2\) kaon-mixing lane already implemented in the repository.
In warped or anarchic-flavor models it is sensitive, through a hadronic
long-distance amplitude, to flavor-changing \(Z\), photon-penguin, and heavy
neutral-gauge effects.  Its most immediate catalog role is as an experimental
normalization for the indirect-CP part of \(K_L\to\pi^0 e^+e^-\), so it should
be kept distinct from short-distance theory prefactors or NDA normalizations.
sections.post_2008:
No post-2008 measurement was found that supersedes the NA48/1 value used by
PDG.  The important developments are interpretive: the rare-kaon Snowmass
white paper emphasizes \(K_{L,S}\to\pi^0\ell^+\ell^-\) branching ratios and
\(q^2\) distributions as new-physics probes, with correlations to
\(\varepsilon'/\varepsilon\), \(\varepsilon_K\), and \(\Delta M_K\).  The
modern literature also treats this \(K_S\) input as part of the indirect-CP
normalization for the \(K_L\) modes rather than as a standalone clean Wilson
coefficient bound (\texttt{aebischer\_buras\_kumar\_2022\_arxiv.txt},
\texttt{isidori\_smith\_unterdorfer\_2004\_arxiv.txt}).
sections.validity:
Class: long-distance-limited rare kaon decay.  The measured partial branching
fraction is robust, but the full-kinematic number is model-dependent because
it assumes a vector matrix element and a unit form factor.  A model constraint
must specify whether it uses the directly measured \(m_{ee}>0.165\) GeV region
or the extrapolated total rate, and whether it predicts the chiral
long-distance amplitude or only a short-distance \(s\to d e^+e^-\) Wilson
coefficient.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  Focused greps over \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/} found no \(K_S\to\pi^0 e^+e^-\), \(S012.10\),
\(a_S\), or \(s\to d e^+e^-\) implementation.  The nearest kaon coverage is
\(\Delta F=2\): \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209} starts the default
neutral-meson-mixing inputs, \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:755}
evaluates \(\Delta m_K\), and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23} lists only
\(\varepsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D0\) policy systems.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Production integration would need a new \(\Delta S=1\)
semileptonic rare-kaon observable, Wilson normalization for \(s\to d e^+e^-\),
and a documented treatment of the \(K_S\) form factor or a conservative
experimental-input-only interpretation.  The existing SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR
\(\Delta F=2\) basis does not cover this decay.
